author: Saltpeter
text:
prime drives sync after the prime chain

checkout::prime ran substrate + the prime chain (adopt/found) and returned without ever fetching, so on an established/joined checkout 'bl prime' did not pull current task state (§12/§13 gap A). After the prime chain, prime now drives the sync chain against the store, reusing prime's binding (config tasks_branch + resolved remote = exactly a no-arg sync) so it calls the sync primitive, never a reimplemented fetch. Idempotent in stealth/just-founded cases.
